page: Amazon Web Services g5.16xlarge is a graphics-intensive virtual machine instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 64 dedicated vCPUs and 32 physical cores provided by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor operating at 3.3 GHz. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, a single NVIDIA Ampere A10G GPU with 22 GB of VRAM, and 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Networking capabilities include a baseline bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate a distinct performance profile. While single-core CPU performance is rated as poor in stress-ng tests, multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth fall within the average range. Conversely, the instance demonstrates strong capabilities in PassMark CPU and memory evaluations, alongside strong Geekbench ray tracing performance. In language model tasks, the server achieves top-tier results for prompt processing and text generation with small (135M) and medium (7B) models. However, a notable performance tradeoff is observed with large (70B) models, where text generation speed drops to a weak rating of 2.72 tokens per second despite strong prompt processing.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the g5.16xlarge bundles high-density GPU acceleration, local NVMe storage, and a high vCPU count, making it a resource-dense option for specialized workloads without requiring separate storage provisioning. This hardware composition is highly suited for GPU-accelerated applications, machine learning inference for small-to-medium models, ray tracing, and data-intensive tasks requiring fast local storage.
description: Amazon Web Services g5.16xlarge is a graphics-intensive instance featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor running at 3.3 GHz, 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. It includes a bundled NVIDIA Ampere A10G GPU with 22 GB of VRAM and 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Performance benchmarks show a contrast between poor single-core CPU metrics and top-tier LLM inference speeds for small and medium models. While PassMark CPU and memory scores are strong, text generation for large 70B models is weak. This configuration offers qualitative cost efficiency by combining dedicated compute, high-speed local storage, and GPU acceleration on the Nitro hypervisor. It is best suited for machine learning inference, ray tracing, and workloads requiring high-density GPU and storage resources.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g5.16xlarge is a graphics-intensive instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, a 1900 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA Ampere A10G GPU with 22 GB VRAM for accelerated workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g5.16xlarge features 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, a 1900 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA A10G GPU for graphics and machine learning.
tagline: An accelerated virtual server combining dedicated AMD EPYC processors, local NVMe storage, and NVIDIA Ampere graphics for demanding machine learning workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 256 GB of DDR4 memory
Bundled NVIDIA Ampere A10G GPU with 22 GB of VRAM
1900 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier prompt processing and text generation for small and medium LLMs
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the Nitro hypervisor
Well-suited for ray tracing, machine learning inference, and graphics-intensive applications
